surface.name: Bird Send your first email through deterministic sandbox delivery
surface.selection_basis: The current documentation home labels Email as its canonical first-send quickstart, while the SMS and phone-number Verify product paths are still marked as coming later. Send your first email explicitly calls itself the fastest path from zero to a delivered email.

alternatives_considered.name: Bird CLI email quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a peer implementation that adds CLI installation and browser OAuth; the main quickstart publishes the shorter raw HTTP route.

alternatives_considered.name: Bird Python email quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This adds Python and SDK installation while reaching the same sandbox result.

alternatives_considered.name: Bird SMS
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The documentation home says the SMS use-case quickstart is coming later, whereas Email has a complete canonical happy path.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Bird account and workspace, then use the official cURL quickstart until the sandbox message reaches delivered status.
assumed_prior_state: No Bird account, organization, workspace, verified email, API key, sending domain, billing method, or message is assumed.
A work-email inbox, password, shell, and cURL are available; no signup, terms acceptance, key creation, or email API request was performed during research.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A deterministic sandbox email sent through Bird's shared onboarding domain is accepted and then reports delivered status without reaching a real mailbox.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Polling the message by its `em_` ID returns `status: delivered` for `delivered@messagebird.dev`.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The quickstart shows the status moving from accepted through processed to delivered, and the sandbox guide defines `email.delivered` as the deterministic happy-path event.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The vendor names this its first-email quickstart and demonstrates delivered as its final verification state before production domain, real-recipient, webhook, and SDK work.

prerequisites.requirement: Use a work email address that can receive Bird's verification message.
Signing up signifies agreement to Bird's Terms and Privacy Policy.
Verify the signup email by entering its code or following its confirmation link.
The account creator starts as organization owner, which can create workspace API keys; Admin and Developer workspace roles can also create keys.
Create a regional API key with `emails:write` and copy its one-time full value.
Have a shell with cURL for the main raw HTTP quickstart.
The free plan requires no credit card; the shared onboarding domain and sandbox recipient avoid production sender setup.

primary_path.action: Open Bird documentation and select the Email first-send quickstart.
primary_path.output: Send your first email opens as the current canonical product happy path.
primary_path.success_signal: The page identifies an API-key-to-delivered-email path.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The SMS and phone-number Verify product paths are marked as coming later; a separate Verify your first customer use-case link is also present.

primary_path.action: Open Bird signup and select Use password instead for the documented work-email-and-password route.
details: Google, GitHub, and email sign-in-link routes are also exposed.
primary_path.output: The password-based signup route is selected.
primary_path.success_signal: The route matches Bird's account-creation guide.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting a different identity route changes the intermediate authentication steps.

primary_path.action: Enter the work email and password and submit signup.
details: Submitting signifies agreement to Bird's Terms and Privacy Policy.
primary_path.output: Bird creates the account and sends a verification code by email.
primary_path.success_signal: The confirmation screen requests email verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The account exists but has limited use until the email is confirmed.

primary_path.action: Open Bird's verification email and enter its code, or follow its confirmation link.
primary_path.output: The email is confirmed and the developer is signed in.
primary_path.success_signal: The Bird dashboard opens for the first time.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the message has not arrived within a minute or two, check spam or use Resend on the confirmation screen.

primary_path.action: Land in the automatically available workspace.
primary_path.output: A ready-to-use workspace exposes Developers, API keys, and the shared onboarding sender.
primary_path.success_signal: The workspace dashboard is visible without DNS or domain verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No setup wait is documented for the shared onboarding domain.

primary_path.action: Open Developers, API keys, choose to create a key, name it, and grant `emails:write`.
primary_path.input: A job-oriented name and the emails write scope
primary_path.output: Bird creates a workspace-bound regional key.
primary_path.success_signal: The full key beginning `bk_us1_` or `bk_eu1_` is displayed once.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A key without the required permission returns 403 on the send endpoint.

primary_path.action: Copy the full key immediately and export it as `BIRD_API_KEY`.
primary_path.output: The shell holds the credential for the quickstart.
primary_path.success_signal: The key prefix identifies the matching regional API host.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A lost key cannot be recovered; create a replacement and revoke the old key.

primary_path.action: POST the published email payload to the host matching the key's region.
details: Use `onboarding@messagebird.dev` as sender and `delivered@messagebird.dev` as recipient.
primary_path.input: Bearer key plus the quickstart from, to, subject, and HTML body
primary_path.output: Bird returns HTTP 202 with an `em_` message ID and `status: accepted`.
primary_path.success_signal: The request is durably accepted for asynchronous processing.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A key sent to the wrong regional host returns 421; an invalid key returns 401; unsupported fields return 422.

primary_path.action: Process the accepted sandbox email asynchronously through the delivery-status pipeline.
primary_path.output: The status advances from accepted through processed toward delivered.
primary_path.success_signal: The deterministic delivered sandbox address selects the happy-path outcome.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The shared sender is capped at roughly 50 recipients per organization per day; exceeding the cap returns 429.

primary_path.action: GET the message by its returned `em_` ID until its status is delivered.
primary_path.input: GET /v1/email/messages/{message_id} with the same bearer key
primary_path.success_signal: The deterministic first-email terminal is observable without a real mailbox.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The sandbox never hands the message to real delivery infrastructure; delivered is the documented simulated outcome in the real event pipeline.

branches.condition: Use Google, GitHub, or an emailed sign-in link
branches.path: Select the corresponding signup control and complete that identity route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes account authentication while converging on the same verified workspace; the account guide selects work email and password for its documented route.

branches.condition: Use the confirmation link instead of the code
branches.path: Follow the link in Bird's verification email.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces code entry but reaches the same signed-in dashboard.

branches.path: Use the `eu1.platform.bird.com` host for the send and status requests.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes only the regional host selected by the `bk_eu1_` prefix.

branches.path: Install `@messagebird/sdk`, construct `BirdClient`, call `bird.email.send`, and then fetch the message by ID.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds Node and package installation but reaches the same accepted and delivered statuses.

branches.path: Replace the recipient with bounce, softbounce, complaint, suppressed, reject, or deferred at `messagebird.dev`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Produces a deterministic non-delivered terminal and therefore does not satisfy the selected happy path.

friction_gates.description: Submitting signup signifies agreement to Bird's Terms and Privacy Policy.
Email confirmation is required before the account has normal dashboard use.
Key creation requires an owner, Admin, or Developer role, and the first-send key needs `emails:write`.
The full API key is displayed only once and must be copied immediately.
The API host must match the region encoded in the key prefix or the request returns 421.
HTTP 202 means accepted, not delivered; the status must progress asynchronously before verification.
The shared onboarding sender is capped at roughly 50 recipients per organization per day and returns 429 above the cap.

time_to_first_success.value: The documentation home describes the Email quickstart as API key to delivered email in under 5 minutes; this does not state an account-signup-to-delivery duration.

sources.title: Create an account & workspace
sections_used: Send a test email - no setup required

sources.title: Sign up | Bird
evidence_supported: Google, GitHub, email-link, and password choices

sources.title: Test email delivery (mail sandbox)
sections_used: What the sandbox does and doesn't do

sources.title: Users, teams & roles
evidence_supported: Admin and Developer API-key and email permissions

sources.title: Plans
sections_used: The pricing model in five lines

excluded_after_success: Verifying a production sending domain, publishing DNS records, sending to real recipients, payment upgrades, and dedicated IPs occur after the deterministic delivered sandbox result.
Webhooks, SDK and CLI variants, batches, contacts, audiences, production retries, tracking, and other messaging channels occur after the first delivered-status check.
